commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh There went up a smoke out of his nostrils, and fire out of his mouth devoured: coals were kindled by it. Augustine of Hippo 9. "There went up smoke in His wrath" (ver. The tearful supplication of penitents went up, when they came to know God's threatenings against the ungodly. "And fire burneth from His face. " And the ardour of love after repentance burns by the knowledge of Him. "Coals were kindled from Him. " They, who were already dead, abandoned by the fire of good desire and the light of righteousness, and who remained in coldness and darkness, re-enkindled and enlightened, have come to life again.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
By it. This relates to the clouds, thunder, and lightning. (Muis)
God's wrath is compared with smoke, fire, a dark night, or mist. (Worthington)
